questions.prompt: A person buys a product because the advertisement includes detailed statistics and scientific evidence about its effectiveness. Which persuasion route best explains this?
A person buys the same product mainly because their favorite celebrity appears in the advertisement. Which persuasion route explains this decision?
A student agrees with the group’s answer during a confusing exam review because they believe the other students understand the material better. Which type of influence is operating?
A student agrees with the group’s incorrect answer because they do not want to appear foolish in front of classmates. Which type of influence is this?
A discussion group about immigration gradually adopts more extreme political positions after members discuss their shared views. Which process explains this?
A committee ignores warning signs about a risky decision because members want to maintain harmony and avoid disagreement. Which process best explains this?
A worker contributes less effort during a group project because individual contributions are not evaluated separately. Which concept explains this?
Fans in a large masked crowd behave aggressively during a riot because they feel anonymous and less personally responsible. Which concept explains this behavior?
A musician performs better in front of an audience when playing a well-practiced song but performs worse when playing a difficult new piece. Which concept explains this?
A person helps someone pick up dropped groceries even though they expect no reward and no one is watching. Which concept best describes this behavior?
A person volunteers at a charity event mainly because it makes them feel happier and improves their mood. Which explanation best fits?
A person helps someone after feeling guilty about ignoring a previous request for help. Which explanation best fits?
A person is less likely to help someone in distress when many other bystanders are present because responsibility feels shared. Which phenomenon explains this?
A person helps their sibling financially but refuses to help a stranger in the same situation because helping relatives increases genetic survival. Which theory explains this?
A person spreads damaging rumors to harm another person's social relationships rather than physically attacking them. Which type of aggression is this?
A violent attack occurs because the attacker is extremely angry and wants to harm the victim. Which type of aggression is this?
A robber threatens someone with violence in order to steal money. Which type of aggression is this?
Children imitate aggressive behavior after watching an adult act aggressively toward a toy in an experiment. Which theory explains this?
A person yells at their coworker after being insulted earlier by someone else. Which aggression process explains redirecting anger toward a safer target?
A program teaching empathy, emotional regulation, and conflict resolution skills to children aims to stop violence before it occurs. Which level of prevention is this?
